2604.24572.title: FastOMOP: A Foundational Architecture for Reliable Agentic Real-World Evidence Generation on OMOP CDM data
2604.24572.paper_summary: The Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership Common Data Model (OMOP CDM), maintained by the Observational Health Data Sciences and Informatics (OHDSI) collaboration, enabled the harmonisation of electronic health records data of nearly one billion patients in 83 countries. Yet generating real-world evidence (RWE) from these repositories remains a manual process requiring clinical, epidemiological and technical expertise. LLMs and multi-agent systems have shown promise for clinical tasks, but RWE automation exposes a fundamental challenge: agentic systems introduce emergent behaviours, coordination failures and safety risks that existing approaches fail to govern. No infrastructure exists to ensure agentic RWE generation is flexible, safe and auditable across the lifecycle. We introduce FastOMOP, an open-source multi-agent architecture that addresses this gap by separating three infrastructure layers, governance, observability and orchestration, from pluggable agent-teams. Governance is enforced at the process boundary through deterministic validation independent of agent reasoning, ensuring no compromised or hallucinating agent can bypass safety controls. Agent teams for phenotyping, study design and statistical analysis inherit these guarantees through controlled tool exposure. We validated FastOMOP using a natural-language-to-SQL agent team across three OMOP CDM datasets: synthetic data from Synthea, MIMIC-IV and a real-world NHS dataset from Lancashire Teaching Hospitals (IDRIL). FastOMOP achieved reliability scores of 0.84-0.94 with perfect adversarial and out-of-scope block rates, demonstrating process-boundary governance delivers safety guarantees independent of model choice. These results indicate that the reliability gap in RWE deployment is architectural rather than model capability, and establish FastOMOP as a governed architecture for progressive RWE automation.
2604.24572.paper_summary_zh: 觀察醫療結果夥伴關聯通用數據模型（OMOP CDM），由觀察健康數據科學與資訊學（OHDSI）合作維護，使得83個國家近十億患者的電子健康紀錄數據得以協調。然而，從這些資料庫生成真實世界證據（RWE）仍然是一個需要臨床、流行病學和技術專業知識的手動過程。大型語言模型（LLMs）和多代理系統在臨床任務中顯示出潛力，但RWE自動化暴露出一個根本性挑戰：代理系統引入了突現行為、協調失敗和安全風險，而現有方法無法加以管理。沒有基礎設施確保代理RWE生成在整個生命週期中是靈活、安全且可審計的。我們介紹了FastOMOP，一個開源的多代理架構，通過將治理、可觀察性和編排三個基礎設施層與可插拔的代理團隊分開來解決這一空白。治理在過程邊界通過獨立於代理推理的確定性驗證得以強化，確保沒有被妥協或產生幻覺的代理能夠繞過安全控制。用於表型、研究設計和統計分析的代理團隊通過受控工具的暴露繼承了這些保證。我們使用一個自然語言到SQL的代理團隊在三個OMOP CDM數據集上驗證了FastOMOP：來自Synthea的合成數據、MIMIC-IV以及來自蘭開夏教學醫院（IDRIL）的真實世界NHS數據集。FastOMOP實現了0.84-0.94的可靠性分數，並且完美地達到了對抗性和超出範疇的阻擋率，顯示出過程邊界治理提供了獨立於模型選擇的安全保證。這些結果表明，RWE部署中的可靠性差距是架構性而非模型能力的問題，並確立了FastOMOP作為進步RWE自動化的治理架構。
2604.24572.author: Niko Moeller-Grell et.al.
2604.24572.authors: Niko Moeller-Grell, Shihao Shenzhang, Zhangshu Joshua Jiang, Richard JB Dobson, Vishnu V Chandrabalan
